rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2019 on Line 1, PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings The Federal Arbitration Act does not apply to "contracts of workers…engaged in foreign or interstate commerce." (9 U.S.C. §1.) Respondent Labor Commissioner Julie Su requests "limited discovery to develop facts relevant to the extent to which Uber drivers engage in interstate commerce." (See Singh v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (3rd Cir. 2019) 939 F.3d 210; Sienkaniec v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (D. Minn. 2019) No. 17-CV-4489 (PSJ/HB) 2019 WL 4308555, at *1.) Commissioner Su's request is granted and Uber Technologies, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is ordered off calendar. The limited-discovery period shall end on March 20, 2020. Uber may then re-calendar its petition for hearing. The parties may request supplemental briefing at that time. The proceedings are otherwise stayed until further order of this court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Cms Security, Incorporated For Good Faith Settlement Determination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Mark C. Phillips And Adam H. Braun
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2019 on Line 2, DEFENDANT CMS SECURITY, INCORPORATED Motion By Cms Security, Incorporated For Good Faith Settlement Determination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Mark C. Phillips And Adam H. Braun CMS Security, Inc.'s motion for good faith settlement determination is granted. CMS's $450,000 settlement with plaintiff - reached with a neutral mediator - is in the Tech-Bilt ballpark. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 494-97; CCP §877.6.) In contrast, Pour Guys SOMA LLC, which opposes the motion, is paying plaintiff $350,000, though its off-duty employee is the person who stabbed plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CMS is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: CHAO HONG TAN VS. JUSTIN CHEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS CHAO HONG TAN, CHAO TANs'Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear. Defendants' demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint was substantially overruled. Third Amended Complaint reflects the scope of plaintiff's claims. Plaintiff pursues allegations the decedent intended for the funds and property in dispute to go to her and that the funds are sufficiently identifiable to achieve that result. Some level of financial discovery is relevant and in that context the Court intends to rule on defendants' objections. To the extent financial discovery is permitted defendants' privacy will be protected. The Court proposes to impose a focused Protective Order initially limiting review of financial documents and discovery responses to attorney eyes only. Both parties are directed to bring to the hearing a copy of all Requests for Admissions referenced in the motion to compel and responses thereto. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BINGSEN LI VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Monetary Sanction Order Of April 15, 2019 Entered Against Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF BINGSEN LI'S Motion To Set Aside Monetary Sanction Order Of April 15, 2019 Entered Against Deceased Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion by plaintiff to set aside the monetary sanction imposed by Judge Pro Tem Julia Campins in connection with the Discovery Order dated April 15, 2019 is denied. Plaintiff is deceased having passed away on October 26, 2018. No successor in interest to the deceased plaintiff has yet been appointed. The basis for denial of C.C.P. 473 relief includes the fact it was not sought within six months following entry of the Discovery Order. See C.C.P. 473(b). Noted is the Discovery Order specifically imposed a monetary sanction against plaintiff Bingsen Li and not his counsel. To the extent the defendants' seek the sanction payment directly from plaintiff's counsel as referenced in the opposition to the motion the request is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2019, Line 6, Defendant Loop Ai Labs Inc.'S Motion For Sanctions Against Anna Gatti For Misappropriation Of Loop-Ais Attorneys Eyes Only And Privileged Materials And For Unlawfully Tampering With Protected Documents; Application For Evidentiary Hearing. Ms. Gatti shall explain her deposition testimony that "Ms. Brayer represented to me that the protective order was terminated by the federal court" and her sworn statement to the contrary in her November 13, 2019 declaration. Assuming the Court orders the oral deposition of Janet Brayer to proceed based on Anna Gatti having waived the attorney-client privilege in her deposition testimony the Court will not rule on the scope of deposition issues as that issue is the subject of a motion set for hearing on December 10, 2019. Alternatively the Court intends to provide guidance to the parties as to the deposition topics permitted to be covered. rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions Against Anna Gatti For Misappropriation Of Loop-Ais Attorneys Eyes Only And Privileged Materials And For Unlawfully Tampering With Protected Documents; Application For Evidentiary Hearing.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT LOOP AI LABS INC.'S Motion For Sanctions Against Anna Gatti For Misappropriation Of Loop-Ais Attorneys Eyes Only And Privileged Materials And For Unlawfully Tampering With Protected Documents; Application For Evidentiary Hearing. (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties may appear by phone. Attorney Janet Brayer shall appear in person. She will clarify at the commencement of the hearing if she is specially appearing solely for herself or specially for plaintiff, Anna Gatti, or for her client in the now dismissed United States District Court (USDC) action. The Court will hear argument on all issues remaining to be decided and will permit further argument relating to the Discovery Order entered November 15, 2019. Plaintiff Anna Gatti and defendant AL Labs shall specifically identify the documents attached to the MSJ motion alleged to have been excluded from attorney client privilege protection by the United States District Court (USDC) order dated May 13, 2016. They shall also identify all documents attached to the MSJ motion alleged to have been protected by the USDC Protective Order. Ms. Gatti shall identify all other confidential documents not attached to the MSJ motion presently in her possession. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel third-party to respond to a document subpoena served on October 30, 2018 is unopposed and granted. DLW Construction, Inc. shall produce the documents identified in the third party subpoena within twenty days following both personal and mail service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. A copy of the subpoena with its proofs of service shall be attached to the Order as an exhibit. Because this motion to compel was brought more than one year after service of the subpoena the request for monetary sanctions is denied. If the third party fails to timely respond to the Order a motion for sanctions may be filed for both disobedience of the subpoena and this Order. rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions As Well As Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3,010 Against Plaintiff, Including Attorneys Fees And Costs, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2019 on Line 4, DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION Motion For Terminating Sanctions As Well As Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of 3,010 Against Plaintiff, Including Attorneys Fees And Costs, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 A hearing on Costco Wholesale Corp.'s motion for sanctions is required. Counsel are to be prepared to discuss, among other things, the following: On August 20, 2019, the court issued an order sustaining Costco's demurrers to plaintiff's complaint. As to three putative causes of action, the demurrers were sustained without leave to amend. As to one cause of action - the third - the demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. On September 4, 2019, Costco served notice of the court order sustaining the demurrers on plaintiff, who then had 10 days to amend. (CRC 3.1320(g).) No amended pleading has been filed. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH A. SOTO VS. PARC 55 LESSEE LLC DBA PARC 55 SAN FRANCISCO - ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2019 on Line 6, DEFENDANT P55 HOTEL OWNER LLC, PARC 55 LESSEE LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT I rule on defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) as follows: Negligence Per Se. "Under the doctrine of negligence per se, the plaintiff 'borrows' statutes to prove duty of care and standard of care." (David v. Hernandez (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 578, 584.) The FAC adequately pleads the negligence per se doctrine; the demurrer is overruled. Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. This count of the FAC does little more than recite the tort's elements from a jury instruction (id. at 14:6-20); the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
